# schema.md — Backtest Data Reference (v5.2)

> Authoritative schema for `backtest/Log.csv`, `backtest/Trades.csv`, and `backtest/TicData.csv`. Every analysis script reads this file before parsing. When the real data disagrees with this spec, **this file is updated first**, and the scripts are re-run against the corrected spec — never the other way around.

---

## 1. Log.csv — the main event log

**Format:** long-format CSV. One event per row. Row meaning depends on the `Tag` column.

**Generated by:** `ModularStrategy/StrategyLogger.cs`

### 1.1 Columns (22, in order)

| # | Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Timestamp | datetime | Bar close time. May be `0001-01-01 00:00:00` on some WARN / ZONE_MITIGATED rows (carry forward during parse). |
| 2 | Tag | string | Row type discriminator. See §1.2. |
| 3 | Bar | int | NinjaTrader bar index. `0` on lifecycle events that aren't bar-aligned (STOP_MOVE, ENTRY_FILL, T1_HIT, T2_HIT, TRADE_RESET, ZONE_MITIGATED, TA_DECISION, and most WARN sub-types). |
| 4 | Direction | string | `Long` / `Short` / (empty). On exit ENTRY_FILLs, reflects the direction of the UNDERLYING trade. |
| 5 | Source | string | Signal category (e.g. `ORB`, `SMF_Impulse`, `VWAP_Reclaim`, `Confluence`). |
| 6 | ConditionSetId | string | Specific logic version (e.g. `ORB_Value_v2`). On `TA_DECISION` rows this holds a trade_id-like label: `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}` (no HH_MM — differs from standard signal_id). |
| 7 | Score | int | Confluence score 0–100 (may exceed 100 on EVAL rows before capping). |
| 8 | Grade | string | `A+` / `A` / `B` / `C`. |
| 9 | Contracts | int | Position size. Populated on ORDER_LMT, ENTRY_FILL, TRADE_RESET. |
| 10 | EntryPrice | float | Intended limit entry on SIGNAL_* / ORDER_LMT; **actual fill price on ENTRY_FILL, including EXIT fills** — the column name is misleading (see §1.3 ENTRY_FILL). |
| 11 | StopPrice | float | Hard stop on signal rows; **new** stop level on STOP_MOVE; final stop on TRADE_RESET. |
| 12 | StopTicks | float | Entry → Stop distance in ticks. |
| 13 | T1Price | float | Partial target on signal rows; T1 level on T1_HIT. |
| 14 | T2Price | float | Runner target on signal rows; T2 level on T2_HIT. |
| 15 | RRRatio | float | T1 distance / stop distance. |
| 16 | ExitPrice | float | 0 while open; populated on T1_HIT / T2_HIT. |
| 17 | PnL | float | 0 while open; realized on T1_HIT / T2_HIT. |
| 18 | SessionPnL | float | Cumulative session P&L at this event. |
| 19 | GateReason | string | Rejection reason on SIGNAL_REJECTED; format: `G<id>:<GateName>(<expression>)`. |
| 20 | Label | string | Multi-purpose. Human name on signal rows; `signal_id` on TOUCH_OUTCOME; disambiguates ENTRY_FILL subtype (`{signal_id}` / `Stop` / `T2` / `NoOvernight` / `SessionClose`); decision on TA_DECISION; `BULL`/`BEAR` on ZONE_MITIGATED. |
| 21 | Detail | string | Tag-dependent payload. See §1.3. |

### 1.2 Tag values (19 known types)

Grouped by role in the strategy lifecycle.

**System:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `SESSION` | Session open/close |

**Signal-generation funnel:**
| Tag | When | Key populated |
|---|---|---|
| `EVAL` | Every candidate signal evaluation | Score, direction, prices |
| `SIGNAL_ACCEPTED` | Signal passed gate filter | Score, Grade, prices, Contracts |
| `SIGNAL_REJECTED` | Signal blocked by a gate | GateReason populated |

**Signal-time snapshots & forensics:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `TOUCH` | Signal-time deep market snapshot |
| `BAR_CONTEXT` | At signal time — 5 bars before |
| `BAR_FORWARD` | After 5 bars — 5 bars after |
| `TOUCH_OUTCOME` | After outcome window (includes :REJ: for counterfactuals) |

**Per-bar market state:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `FLOW` | Every bar |
| `STRUCT` | Every bar |

**Trade-lifecycle (post-acceptance execution):**
| Tag | When | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `ORDER_LMT` | Limit order placed after SIGNAL_ACCEPTED | Marks order submission |
| `ENTRY_FILL` | ANY fill event (entry OR exit — name is misleading) | Position opened (entry) or closed (exit); Label disambiguates |
| `TA_DECISION` | Per-bar trade-mgmt reasoning while position open | Decision audit |
| `STOP_MOVE` | Stop level adjusted (BE arm / trail / MFE lock) | Order-management event |
| `T1_HIT` | Target 1 partial fill | Partial exit |
| `T2_HIT` | Target 2 runner fill | Runner exit |
| `TRADE_RESET` | Position fully closed | Final trade state |

**Diagnostics:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `WARN` | Diagnostic events (sub-typed — 15 known variants; see §1.3) |
| `ZONE_MITIGATED` | Zone invalidated |

### 1.3 Detail-column parsing specifications

#### Context string (on EVAL, SIGNAL_ACCEPTED, SIGNAL_REJECTED)

```
h4=[+|-|0] h2=[+|-|0] h1=[+|-|0] smf=[bull|bear|neutral|flat]
str=<float> sw=<int>
```

#### GateReason (on SIGNAL_REJECTED only)

```
G<id>:<GateName>(<expression>)
e.g.  G3.5:ThinMarket(vt=150<400=0.40×1000)
```

Parse into `gate_id`, `gate_name`, `gate_expression`.

#### TOUCH_OUTCOME Detail

```
MFE=<f> MAE=<f> HIT_STOP=<0|1> HIT_TARGET=<0|1>
FIRST_HIT=<TARGET|STOP|NONE> SIM_PNL=<f>
BARS_TO_HIT=<i> CLOSE_END=<f> WINDOW_BARS=<i>
```

#### BAR_CONTEXT Detail (5 segments, `|` delimiter NO SPACES, oldest first)

**Delimiter:** single `|` character, **no surrounding spaces**. Verified in v5.2. Each Detail contains exactly 5 pipe-separated segments.

```
O:<f> H:<f> L:<f> C:<f> V:<int> D:<int>|O:...|O:...|O:...|O:...
```

Bar offsets `-5` (oldest) → `-1` (most recent before signal).

#### BAR_FORWARD Detail (5 segments, `|` delimiter NO SPACES, earliest first)

**Delimiter:** single `|` character, **no surrounding spaces**. Verified in v5.2. Each Detail contains exactly 5 pipe-separated segments with timestamp prefix on each.

```
T:<HH:MM> O:<f> H:<f> L:<f> C:<f> V:<int> D:<int>|T:<HH:MM> O:...|...
```

Bar offsets `+1` → `+5` after the signal bar.

**Note:** timestamps are `HH:MM` format, not ISO — they're bar times on the signal's day.

#### FLOW Detail

Groups separated by ` | ` (pipe WITH spaces — different from BAR_*).

```
REGIME=<+1|0|-1> STR=<f>
| BD=<int> CD=<int> DSL=<int> DSH=<int>
| DEX=<+1|0|-1> BDIV=<0|1> BERDIV=<0|1>
| ABS=<f> SBULL=<int> SBEAR=<int>
| IZB=<0|1> IZS=<0|1>
| HASVOL=<0|1> SW=<int>
| TRD=<int> CH_L=<0|1> CH_S=<0|1> BOS_L=<0|1> BOS_S=<0|1>
```

#### STRUCT Detail

Groups separated by ` | ` (pipe WITH spaces).

```
POC=<f> VAH=<f> VAL=<f>
| NEAR_SUP=<f>(<f>t) NEAR_RES=<f>(<f>t)
| H4=<high>/<low> H1=<high>/<low>
| PP=<f> SKEW=<f>
```

#### TOUCH Detail

Groups separated by ` | ` (pipe WITH spaces).

```
ZONE_TYPE=<OB|FVG_BULL|FVG_BEAR|NONE> ZONE_LO=<f> ZONE_HI=<f>
| TRD=<+1|-1> BOS_L=<0|1>
| FVG_BL=<0|1> FVG_BL_LO=<f> FVG_BL_HI=<f>
| BD=<int> CD=<int> ABS=<f> SBULL=<int>
| POC=<f> VAH=<f>
| H4B=<f> BDIV=<0|1> REGIME=<+1|0|-1> ATR=<f> HASVOL=<0|1>
```

**Delimiter inconsistency in the logger:**
- `BAR_CONTEXT` and `BAR_FORWARD` use `|` (no spaces)
- `FLOW`, `STRUCT`, `TOUCH` use ` | ` (with spaces)

This is a logger-style inconsistency worth flagging to your dev team. Parsers must use the correct delimiter per Tag.

---

### 1.3.1 WARN Sub-types (15 known variants — NEW IN v5.2)

WARN's Detail column starts with a sub-type marker. Parse by extracting the first word (before space, `:`, or `[`) and dispatch.

Count from current 3.5-month backtest shown for each. Total WARN rows: 141,555.

#### `RANK_WEAK` (2,492 rows) — rank floor failure

```
RANK_WEAK [<module_id>] A=<int> B=<int> C=<int> D=<int>
                        Pen=<int> Net=<int> Mult=<float>
```

**Purpose:** candidate EVAL failed its rank floor test. Silent filter — no SIGNAL_REJECTED is emitted.

**Layers:** A (MTFA), B (S/R), C (OrderFlow), D (Structure), Pen (penalty).
**Invariant:** `Net == A + B + C + D − Pen`
**Mult:** Net/<max_layer_sum>, informational multiplier.

Join: appears immediately after the EVAL it relates to. Match by `[module_id]` == EVAL's `ConditionSetId` at same Bar.

#### `RANK_WIN` (1,239 rows) — rank passed

```
RANK_WIN [<module_id>] Raw=<int> A=<int> B=<int> C=<int> D=<int>
                       Pen=<int> Net=<int> Mult=<float>
                       | A:<tokens> B:<tokens> C:<tokens> D:<tokens>
                       Final=<float>
```

**Purpose:** candidate EVAL passed rank evaluation. Emitted once per signal that proceeds to the gate stage. Appears immediately after its EVAL.

**Fields beyond RANK_WEAK:**
- `Raw` = pre-layer-processing score
- `Final` = post-multiplier final score
- Token breakdown after `|`: which conditions fired inside each layer
  - `A:h4+ h2+` etc — layer A token contributions
  - `B:touch+s25` — layer B (support/resistance at 25-tick distance)
  - `C:bd+vwap+bp+` — order flow components
  - `D:LH+LL` / `D:trend` / `D:choch` — structure patterns

**This is a gold mine for Phase 24 score calibration** — we have raw, layer-decomposed, and final scores per signal.

**Count reconciliation:** `RANK_WIN = 1,239 = SIGNAL_ACCEPTED (795) + SIGNAL_REJECTED (444)`. **Every passing signal gets a RANK_WIN** — so RANK_WIN is the universal pre-gate stamp.

#### `RANK_VETO` (1,879 rows) — rank-stage veto

```
RANK_VETO [<module_id>] <L|S> conf=A=<int> B=<int> C=<int> D=<int>
                              Pen=<int> Net=<int> Mult=<float> VETOED
```

**Purpose:** candidate passed rank floor but was **vetoed at the rank stage** (before gate evaluation). Different from SIGNAL_REJECTED which happens at the gate stage.

**Fields:**
- `L|S` = long/short conviction direction
- `conf=` prefix on the layer breakdown (key difference from RANK_WEAK)
- `Mult=0.00 VETOED` = literal marker; veto overrides score

**This confirms the two-stage funnel:**

```
EVAL
  ├─► RANK_WEAK (2,492)   silent drop (below floor)
  ├─► RANK_VETO (1,879)   silent veto (rank-stage block)
  ├─► RANK_WIN (1,239)    rank cleared
  │      ├─► SIGNAL_REJECTED (444)   gate veto
  │      └─► SIGNAL_ACCEPTED (795)   approved
  │
  └─► (11,130 others)    silently dropped — logging gap
```

**Gap analysis:**
```
17,979 EVAL total
  2,492 RANK_WEAK
  1,879 RANK_VETO
  1,239 RANK_WIN  (which becomes 444 + 795 = 1,239 — matches)
─────────
  5,610 accounted
 12,369 gap (likely intentional early-exit logic without logging)
```

**Logging gap** — 69% of EVALs are filtered silently. Phase 27's funnel audit should flag this and ask the dev team to either add logging for the silent filter stages or document that they're intentional early-exits.

#### `RANK_CONFLICT` (145 rows) — conflict penalty applied

```
RANK_CONFLICT [<module_id>] -<int> penalty applied
```

**Purpose:** informational — a conflict penalty was applied at ranking. The `-15` is the penalty magnitude (matches `PENALTIES['RANK_CONFLICT']` in strategy_config).

Associate with EVAL at same bar + module_id.

#### `SR` (109,800 rows) — S/R zone broken

```
SR zone broken at <price> [<zone_type>] close=<price>
```

**Purpose:** price closed beyond a tracked S/R level, invalidating it. `[Pvt]` = pivot, other types possible.

**High volume** — emitted continuously as price moves through levels. Useful for Phase 25 hygiene analysis (signals firing at recently-broken SR).

Timestamp may be `0001-01-01` — carry forward last valid timestamp.

#### `ORB_DIAG` (13,536 rows) — ORB breakout status

```
ORB_DIAG: <status> ORBH=<f> ORBL=<f>
```

Status values: `no_breakout_yet`, others TBD.

**Purpose:** per-bar ORB state check. Informational.

#### `ORB_VP_DIAG` (8,995 rows) — ORB volume profile bar accumulation

```
ORB_VP_DIAG: bar accumulated levels=<int> vol=<int> time=<timestamp>
```

**Purpose:** per-bar logging of volume-at-price accumulation during ORB. Debugging info.

#### `ORB_MEASURE` (75 rows) — per-day ORB measurements

```
ORB_MEASURE: date=<YYYY-MM-DD>,dir=<Long|Short>,breakoutBar=<int>,
             barsTo<...>=<...>
```

**Purpose:** once-per-session ORB breakout metrics. 75 rows ≈ number of trading days in the backtest.

#### `ORB_VP` (75 rows) — ORB value-area lock per session

```
ORB_VP LOCKED (RTH): POC=<f> VAH=<f> VAL=<f>
```

**Purpose:** final volume-profile values for RTH ORB. One per session. Useful for cross-referencing POC to signal performance.

#### `TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW` (1,419 rows) — shadow-logged TA tighten decisions

```
TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW sid=<signal_id> set=<ConditionSetId> <additional>
```

**Purpose:** **TA engine proposed a TIGHTEN action that was NOT executed.** This is shadow logging — what the system would have done vs what it did.

**Critical finding enabler for Phase 26:** we don't have to infer action-result gaps; every shadow decision is directly logged. Count mismatch between TA_DECISION with `act=Tighten` and actual STOP_MOVE tells the execution gap.

Join to trade via `sid=` (signal_id).

#### `TA_EXIT_SHADOW` (25 rows) — shadow-logged TA exit decisions

```
TA_EXIT_SHADOW sid=<signal_id> set=<ConditionSetId> <additional>
```

**Purpose:** TA engine proposed an EXIT action that was NOT executed. Only 25 of these in 3.5 months — your exit logic is much stickier than your tighten logic.

Join via `sid=`.

#### `SLIP` (795 rows) — per-trade slippage measurement

```
SLIP entry=<float>t exit=<float>t SessionAware
```

**Purpose:** **logged slippage per trade in ticks, at both entry AND exit.** 795 rows = one per entry ENTRY_FILL.

**Format:**
- `entry=4.0t` = 4 ticks of slippage on the entry fill
- `exit=2.5t` = 2.5 ticks slippage on the exit fill
- `SessionAware` = flag that slippage estimate accounts for session

**This is the authoritative slippage source.** Phase 23's slippage audit should read from SLIP rows directly, not reconstruct from `ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice - SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.EntryPrice`.

**Quick aggregate:** 795 × (4.0 + 2.5) ticks = ~5,168 slippage ticks. On MNQ at $0.50/tick × 1 contract: **~$2,584 in slippage over 3.5 months** — larger than entire gross P&L.

Timestamp may be `0001-01-01` — carry forward. Join to trade via proximity (SLIP appears near entry/exit pair).

#### `FP_TRADE_OPEN` (794 rows) — trade-open footprint marker

```
FP_TRADE_OPEN sid=<signal_id> set=<ConditionSetId> <additional fields>
```

**Purpose:** fires exactly when a trade opens. 794 rows = number of successful entries (matches TRADE_RESET count).

Useful as a reliable entry-marker alternative when ENTRY_FILL parsing is ambiguous.

#### `DynamicBE` (248 rows) — dynamic BE-arm trigger reasoning

```
DynamicBE:<mode>(MFE=<float>t>=<pct>%×T1=<float>t) entry=<float>
                 stop_moved_to=<float> <additional>
```

**Example:** `DynamicBE:T1_Prox(MFE=35.0t>=70%×T1=37.4t) entry=25860.75`

**Purpose:** records when and why dynamic BE-arm moved the stop. Shows the threshold condition that fired.

Modes observed: `T1_Prox` (MFE reached percent-of-T1-distance), others possible.

**This is the reasoning log for BE-arm decisions.** Phase 23's BE-arm effectiveness analysis should read DynamicBE directly to understand *why* each BE-arm fired, not just the fact it did.

Join to trade via `entry=` price match against the trade's entry ENTRY_FILL (need to verify — may also contain sid).

#### `G4` (38 rows) — Gate 4 size-floor adjustment

```
G4:SizeFloor — risk=$<n> stop=<n>t×$<n>=$<n> → <n> contract (reason)
```

**Purpose:** Gate 4 enforces contract-size floor based on risk. Logs when the computed size was adjusted.

**Example:** `G4:SizeFloor — risk=$1250 stop=312t×$5=$1559 → 1 contract (reduced)`

Useful for Phase 8 (sizing alignment) analysis.

---

#### SESSION Detail

```
OPEN  VWAP=<f> ATR=<f>
CLOSE VWAP=<f> ATR=<f>
```

#### ZONE_MITIGATED row

- Direction: `Long` or `Short`
- Label: `BULL` or `BEAR`
- Detail: `close=<f> zone=<lo>-<hi>`

---

#### ORDER_LMT Detail

```
LIMIT
```

Literal marker. Order parameters in structured columns.

---

#### ENTRY_FILL — ANY fill event (name is misleading)

**Label disambiguates subtype:**

| Label value                          | event_subtype   | Meaning | Current count |
|--------------------------------------|-----------------|---------|---|
| `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}`  | `entry`         | Position opened | 795 |
| `Stop`                               | `stop_exit`     | Any stop triggered | 701 |
| `T2`                                 | `t2_exit`       | T2 runner exit fill | 80 |
| `NoOvernight`                        | `forced_exit`   | Session-end forced close | 12 |
| `SessionClose`                       | `forced_exit`   | Session-end variant | ~1 |
| **Total**                            |                 |         | **1,588** |

**Note on `SessionClose`:** alternative spelling to `NoOvernight`. Both map to `event_subtype='forced_exit'`. Count both when reconciling.

(Rest of ENTRY_FILL spec unchanged from v5.1.)

**Slippage computation (entries only):**

```
slippage_ticks = (ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice - SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.EntryPrice)
                 × side_sign / tick_size
```

**Cross-check against WARN SLIP row** for the same trade — if SLIP.entry differs by >0.5 ticks from computed slippage, flag discrepancy.

**Realized P&L formula** — see v5.1 ENTRY_FILL section, unchanged.

---

#### TA_DECISION / STOP_MOVE / T1_HIT / T2_HIT / TRADE_RESET Details

Unchanged from v5.1.

### 1.4 Signal identifier format

Unchanged from v5.1. Normalize TA_DECISION variant (`:yyyymmdd:` without `_hhmm`) to the canonical form for joining.

### 1.5 The complete event funnel (v5.2)

```
SESSION OPEN
    │
    ├─► FLOW, STRUCT, ORB_VP_DIAG, ORB_DIAG  (per-bar diagnostics)
    │   SR WARN  (emitted as levels break)
    │   ORB_VP, ORB_MEASURE (once per session)
    │
    └─► EVAL (candidate scored)
          │
          ├─► RANK_WEAK (WARN, ~2,492)      → silent drop (below floor)
          │
          ├─► RANK_VETO (WARN, ~1,879)      → silent veto (rank-stage)
          │
          ├─► RANK_WIN (WARN, ~1,239)       → rank cleared
          │     │
          │     ├─► SIGNAL_REJECTED (444)   → gate vetoed
          │     │     └─► TOUCH + BAR_CONTEXT + BAR_FORWARD + TOUCH_OUTCOME:REJ:
          │     │
          │     └─► SIGNAL_ACCEPTED (795)   → approved
          │           │
          │           ├─► TOUCH + BAR_CONTEXT + BAR_FORWARD + TOUCH_OUTCOME
          │           ├─► RANK_CONFLICT (WARN) if conflict penalty applied
          │           ├─► G4 (WARN) if size floor adjusted
          │           │
          │           └─► ORDER_LMT (794)   → limit placed
          │                 │
          │                 ├─► (cancelled, no fill) → no follow-up
          │                 │
          │                 └─► ENTRY_FILL(entry) (795)   → position opened
          │                       │
          │                       ├─► SLIP (WARN) logs entry slippage
          │                       ├─► FP_TRADE_OPEN (WARN) marks trade start
          │                       │
          │                       ├─► TA_DECISION × N     (per-bar reasoning)
          │                       ├─► TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW × M  (proposed but not executed)
          │                       ├─► TA_EXIT_SHADOW × P     (proposed but not executed)
          │                       ├─► DynamicBE × Q          (BE-arm trigger reasoning)
          │                       ├─► STOP_MOVE × R          (actual stop changes)
          │                       ├─► T1_HIT                 (partial, if hit)
          │                       │
          │                       ├─► ENTRY_FILL(stop_exit) ← OR
          │                       ├─► ENTRY_FILL(t2_exit)   ← OR     ONE
          │                       ├─► ENTRY_FILL(forced_exit) ←      OF
          │                       │                                  THESE
          │                       ├─► T2_HIT                (paired with t2_exit)
          │                       ├─► SLIP (WARN) logs exit slippage (shared with entry row)
          │                       └─► TRADE_RESET           (closes trade)
          │
          └─► (11,130 others)    silently dropped — logging gap, unknown stage

SESSION CLOSE
```

### 1.6 Parsing quirks & conventions

| Quirk | Handling |
|---|---|
| Timestamp `0001-01-01 00:00:00` on most WARN sub-types, ZONE_MITIGATED | Carry forward last valid timestamp. |
| `Bar=0` on lifecycle events | Logger doesn't bar-align these. Use `Timestamp`. |
| `NEAR_SUP=<price>(0.0t)` | Price is AT the level. |
| `POC=0.00 VAH=0.00 VAL=0.00` | Value area not accumulated → NaN, not zero. |
| BAR_CONTEXT / BAR_FORWARD use `|` (no spaces) as delimiter | **Verified v5.2.** Splits to exactly 5 segments. |
| FLOW / STRUCT / TOUCH use ` | ` (pipe WITH spaces) as delimiter | Logger inconsistency — flag to dev team but handle as-is. |
| RANK_WEAK → EVAL association | Match by `[module_id]` + same `Bar`. |
| RANK_WIN is the universal pre-gate stamp | Every SIGNAL_ACCEPTED + SIGNAL_REJECTED has exactly one RANK_WIN. |
| RANK_VETO is distinct from SIGNAL_REJECTED | RANK_VETO = rank-stage veto; SIGNAL_REJECTED = gate-stage veto. Different fail modes. |
| 11,130 EVALs with no follow-up | Logging gap — early-exit logic not producing log entries. Flag in Phase 27. Not a bug per se, but blocks full funnel audit. |
| Theoretical max EVAL Score | `30 + 40 + 30 + 15 = 115`. Above = logger bug. |
| TA_DECISION `ConditionSetId` uses `:yyyymmdd:` not `:yyyymmdd_hhmm:` | Normalize during trade_id join. |
| TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW / TA_EXIT_SHADOW = proposals that didn't execute | Direct measure of TA decision → execution gap. |
| SLIP row emits once per trade containing both entry and exit slippage | Single row, two values. Join to trade via proximity. |
| ENTRY_FILL Label variants: Stop / T2 / NoOvernight / SessionClose | All four are exit fills; map `NoOvernight` and `SessionClose` both to `forced_exit` subtype. |
| DynamicBE Detail uses `:` in `DynamicBE:<mode>(...)` | Parse sub-sub-type from after `DynamicBE:` and before `(`. |

---

## 2. Trades.csv — NinjaTrader trade executions

Unchanged from v5.1. Log.csv is primary; Trades.csv is cross-check only.

---

## 3. TicData.csv — tick-level market data

Unchanged from v5.1.

---

## 4. Instrument parameters

Unchanged from v5.1.

---

## 5. JOIN STRATEGY

### 5.1 Within Log.csv — signal-generation stage (UPDATED v5.2)

Primary key: `signal_id = {ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd_hhmm}:{Bar}`, `:REJ:` for rejected.

- `EVAL ↔ RANK_WEAK`: same `Bar` AND `[module_id]` from WARN Detail equals EVAL's `ConditionSetId`. RANK_WEAK follows EVAL in log order.
- `EVAL ↔ RANK_VETO`: same Bar AND `[module_id]` matches. RANK_VETO follows EVAL in log order. Direction from the `L|S` token must match EVAL's Direction.
- `EVAL ↔ RANK_WIN`: same Bar AND `[module_id]` matches. RANK_WIN is the bridge — every accepted AND rejected signal has a RANK_WIN.
- `RANK_WIN ↔ SIGNAL_*`: same Bar AND ConditionSetId.
- `EVAL ↔ RANK_CONFLICT` (optional): same Bar AND `[module_id]` matches.
- `SIGNAL_* ↔ TOUCH`: `(Bar, Source, ConditionSetId)`. Exactly one TOUCH per SIGNAL_*.
- `SIGNAL_* ↔ BAR_CONTEXT / BAR_FORWARD`: nearest preceding/following with matching `Bar`.
- `SIGNAL_* ↔ TOUCH_OUTCOME`: parse signal_id from `Label`.

### 5.1a Within Log.csv — trade-lifecycle stage (UPDATED v5.2)

Canonical `trade_id` = normalized signal_id of the opening SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.

Joins:

- `SIGNAL_ACCEPTED ↔ ORDER_LMT`: same `(Source, ConditionSetId, Direction)` + ORDER_LMT within 5 minutes after. 1:1 expected.
- `ORDER_LMT ↔ ENTRY_FILL(entry)`: entry ENTRY_FILL's Label contains signal_id directly. Parse it.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ TA_DECISION`: TA_DECISION's `ConditionSetId` carries trade_id variant. Normalize and join.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ STOP_MOVE`: chronological range between entry and next TRADE_RESET sharing (Source, ConditionSetId, Direction).
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ T1_HIT / T2_HIT / exit ENTRY_FILL`: same proximity rule.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ TRADE_RESET`: chronologically next TRADE_RESET.

**NEW in v5.2 — WARN-level joins:**

- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ SLIP WARN`: **SLIP row appears near the entry or shortly after.** Match by proximity (within same bar range) and presence of entry/exit tick values. One SLIP per trade.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ FP_TRADE_OPEN WARN`: SLIP-like proximity; `sid=` in Detail carries signal_id directly.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW / TA_EXIT_SHADOW WARN`: `sid=` contains signal_id (normalized form). Direct key match.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ↔ DynamicBE WARN`: match by `entry=<price>` value against ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice in the trade's time range.
- `EVAL ↔ G4 / RANK_CONFLICT WARN`: same Bar + module_id; these fire during scoring for individual signals.

### 5.1b trade_id normalization

Unchanged from v5.1.

### 5.2 Log.csv → Trades.csv

Sanity-check only.

### 5.3 Log.csv → TicData.csv

Time-window lookup.

---

## 6. Validation checklist (UPDATED v5.2)

1–17: unchanged from v5.1.

**18. RANK sub-type count consistency:**
```
Count(RANK_WIN) == Count(SIGNAL_ACCEPTED) + Count(SIGNAL_REJECTED)
Current: 1,239 == 795 + 444 ✓

Count(RANK_WEAK) + Count(RANK_VETO) + Count(RANK_WIN)
  + silent_gap == Count(EVAL)
Current: 2,492 + 1,879 + 1,239 + 12,369 == 17,979 ✓
  (gap of 12,369 is the known logging gap — see quirks)
```

**19. RANK_WEAK / RANK_WIN Net invariant:**
```
Net == A + B + C + D − Pen
For RANK_WEAK: verify on all rows.
For RANK_WIN:  verify on all rows.
For RANK_VETO: Net computed but Mult=0 forces veto regardless.
```

**20. Delimiter consistency:**
```
BAR_CONTEXT / BAR_FORWARD Detail: assert 5 segments after splitting on '|'
FLOW / STRUCT / TOUCH Detail: assert 6-7 segments after splitting on ' | '
Any deviation = format drift.
```

**21. SLIP row coverage:**
```
Count(SLIP) should match Count(ENTRY_FILL entries).
Current: 795 == 795 ✓
Missing SLIP for a trade means slippage unrecorded for that trade.
```

**22. Shadow-to-actual gap (informational, for Phase 26):**
```
Count(TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW) = 1,419
Count(STOP_MOVE with detail indicating tighten) = ?
Gap = proposals that didn't execute.
```

---

## 7. Version history

| Version | Change | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| v1 | Initial inference | Pre-spec discovery |
| v2 | Canonical 22-column spec, 6 Tags | StrategyLogger.cs shared |
| v3 | Added TOUCH, FLOW, STRUCT + microstructure | Deep-data revealed |
| v4 | Added EVAL, WARN, SESSION, ZONE_MITIGATED + FLOW/STRUCT extensions | Log sample revealed full funnel |
| v5 | Added 7 trade-lifecycle Tags with full Detail specs and trade_id normalization | Phase 0 output revealed 19 Tag types |
| v5.1 | ENTRY_FILL is ANY fill event (entry + stop_exit + t2_exit + forced_exit), disambiguated by Label; added realized P&L formula and T2 reconciliation | ENTRY_FILL Label counts revealed 701 Stop + 80 T2 + 12 NoOvernight + 795 entries = 1588 total |
| v5.2 | Documented all 15 WARN sub-types with Detail parsing specs (RANK_WIN, RANK_VETO, SR, ORB_DIAG, ORB_VP_DIAG, ORB_MEASURE, ORB_VP, TA_TIGHTEN_SHADOW, TA_EXIT_SHADOW, SLIP, FP_TRADE_OPEN, DynamicBE, G4, RANK_CONFLICT in addition to existing RANK_WEAK); corrected BAR_CONTEXT / BAR_FORWARD delimiter to `|` no spaces; documented two-stage filter funnel (RANK → GATE); added SessionClose as exit_subtype variant; added SLIP/FP_TRADE_OPEN/DynamicBE/TA_SHADOW as direct analysis inputs | Gemini's 01d diagnostic revealed 15 sub-types, 12,369 silent-gap EVALs, delimiter correction, and rich shadow-logging previously invisible to analysis |
